title: carduus × stangii nothosubsp. semiperegrinus
intro: Carduus × stangii nothosubsp. semiperegrinus, also known as the intermediate cardoon thistle, is a subspecies of thistle that is native to the Mediterranean region and is characterized by its prickly stems and large, purple flowers. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects, making it useful for treating various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The health benefits of Carduus × stangii nothosubsp. semiperegrinus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can be used externally as a topical cream or ointment to treat skin conditions and wounds.
